title: Unity Q1 2026: Strong Revenue Growth Amid Major Restructuring
text: Financial Performance: Unity Technologies reported impressive financial results for Q1 2026, with revenue reaching $508 million, representing a 17% year-over-year increase from the prior year's $435 million. However, the company posted a substantial net loss of $347 million, primarily driven by one-time charges related to shutting down the controversial IronSource ad network and divesting its Supersonic mobile publisher division. Core Business Momentum: Despite the headline loss, Unity's underlying operations demonstrated robust growth. The Grow Solutions division, which encompasses advertising technology, surged 23% to generate $352 million in revenue. This expansion was largely attributed to the launch of Unity Vector, an AI-powered advertising tool introduced in 2025. The Create Solutions segment, representing the foundational game engine tools developers use, grew more modestly at 4% to reach $157 million, driven primarily by increased subscription income. Strategic Pivot to AI: When excluding restructuring charges, Unity achieved adjusted EBITDA of $138 million with improved free cash flow of $66 million, while maintaining $2.1 billion in cash reserves. The company is now focusing on AI-driven development tools and Unity 6 to rebuild developer confidence following years of controversy surrounding the IronSource partnership and the failed 2023 Runtime Fee policy.
